items.headline: City updates affordability rules for subsidized housing projects
items.summary: San José is clarifying how affordable housing restrictions work if subsidies expire or projects face foreclosure, protecting long-term affordability commitments. This affects existing affordable housing developments across the city.

items.headline: Airport upgrades baggage handling system with new contract
items.summary: The city approved a fifth amendment to extend Vanderlande Industries' baggage conveyor system services at San José Airport. This involves ongoing maintenance and improvements to airport infrastructure.

items.headline: City-wide storm sewer system gets comprehensive master planning update
items.summary: San José is moving forward with a master planning effort for the entire storm sewer system through an amended contract with Wood Rodgers, Inc. This long-term infrastructure planning will guide future flood prevention and drainage improvements.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 housing development underway on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four new homes permitted as part of the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development along Altino Boulevard, with prices ranging from $487k-$574k. This represents a significant wave of new single-family construction in what appears to be a major residential development project.

items.headline: Sherly Place new home construction begins with three units permitted
items.summary: Three new homes permitted at Sherly Place, representing the start of another residential development in San José. Combined with Comm Hill activity, this signals notable housing growth in these neighborhoods.

items.headline: City Council Reviews Capital Improvement Projects for FY 2026-27
items.summary: Mountain View is evaluating its capital spending priorities for the next fiscal year, which typically includes infrastructure, parks, and facilities upgrades. This study session determines what major projects the city will fund over the coming year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates residential tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend tenant protection policies in Chapter 19.71 of the Municipal Code. This change could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in Sunnyvale.
